statutoryBodies.name: UNESCO Scholarship Fund
statutoryBodies.statusNote: Established under Section 2 as a body corporate with perpetual succession and a common seal. Capable of suing and being sued. The Government may donate grants to the Fund. At commencement, all monies from the old Treasury Account No. 6,000-0-0-6-65 (UNESCO Fund) were transferred into this statutory Fund (Section 5).
powers: Award scholarships to disabled and displaced children (Section 8(a))
Receive grants, gifts, and donations — local and foreign (Section 4)
Acquire and dispose of movable property (Section 4)
dataGaps: Current balance of the UNESCO Scholarship Fund
List of educational books published under Section 8(b)
Whether the customs exemption under Section 11 has been exercised

statutoryBodies.name: Board of Management (UNESCO Scholarship Fund)
statutoryBodies.statusNote: Section 3 vests the administration, management, and control of the Fund entirely in the Board of Management. Chaired by the Secretary to the Ministry of Education. Quorum is four members.

exOfficio.role: Secretary to the Ministry of Education
Secretary-General of the Sri Lanka National Commission for UNESCO
Chief Accountant of the Ministry of Education
Director, Department of Child Care & Probation
Deputy Secretary-General, SL National Commission for UNESCO

meetings.convenedBy: Chairman (Secretary to the Ministry of Education)
meetings.chairperson: Secretary to the Ministry of Education (Section 3)
meetings.reporting: Annual report to the Minister, placed before Parliament (Section 9)

powers: Full administration, management, and control of the Fund (Section 3)
Receive grants, gifts, and donations — local and foreign (Section 4)
Acquire and dispose of movable property (Section 4)
Award scholarships to disabled and displaced children (Section 4)
Enter into contracts and appoint personnel (Section 4)
Maintain bank accounts at licensed commercial banks (Section 6)
dataGaps: Current Board membership — whether all ex-officio positions are filled
Meeting frequency and recent meeting dates

statutoryBodies.name: Chief Administrative Officer (UNESCO Scholarship Fund)
statutoryBodies.statusNote: The Secretary-General of the Sri Lanka National Commission for UNESCO serves as the Chief Administrative Officer of the Fund, working under the Board's direction (Section 7).

exOfficio.role: Secretary-General, SL National Commission for UNESCO

powers: Day-to-day administration of the Fund under Board direction (Section 7)
Oversee operational delivery of scholarships and publishing
dataGaps: Staff complement working under the CAO

timeline.details: Act No. 44 of 1999 commenced on December 3, 1999. Established the UNESCO Scholarship Fund as a body corporate to provide scholarships for disabled and displaced children. Existing Treasury funds (Account No. 6,000-0-0-6-65) transferred to the new statutory Fund.

tiers.name: Minister of Education
tiers.relationship: Receives annual report (Section 9); may grant customs exemptions with Finance Minister concurrence (Section 11)

tiers.name: Board of Management
tiers.relationship: Full administration, management, and control of the Fund (Section 3); chaired by Secretary to Ministry of Education

tiers.name: Chief Administrative Officer
tiers.relationship: Secretary-General of UNESCO Commission; day-to-day administration under Board direction (Section 7)

tiers.name: Deputy Secretary-General (Secretary to Board)
tiers.relationship: Secretary to the Board, handles meeting administration (Section 3)

currentReplacement.national: Board of Management chaired by Secretary to Ministry of Education
